narrative: Dear [Student's Name], I know this rejection letter stings, especially after pouring so much into your law school dreams, and I want to walk you through why our predictive model saw your path to the bar exam as riskier than we'd like—landing at a 49% chance of not passing. Picture it like this: the model looks at patterns from thousands of students who've walked this road before, and for you, it zeroed in first on your Year 3 decile of 2, which stood out as the biggest signal pulling toward potential struggles, suggesting those later grades placed you near the bottom of your school's pack in a way that echoes past failures for others. Layered on top was your LSAT score of 27, the second-strongest factor tipping the scales downward, as it falls short of the benchmarks that typically light the way to bar success. Your Year 1 decile of 3 reinforced that early pattern, making the model worry about consistency from the start. Then there's your Asian background, which unfortunately aligned with some tougher historical trends in the data, nudging the prediction further toward risk, followed closely by your low family income bracket that often correlates with added hurdles like fewer resources for prep. Your undergraduate GPA of 3.1 didn't lift things enough to counterbalance, and even being male and full-time added subtle pulls in the same direction. It's this whole picture together—your grades deciling low across years, that LSAT, and the supporting factors—that painted a story of higher odds for bar failure, not any one thing alone. You're clearly driven, aiming straight for law with that full-time commitment, and this isn't a judgment on your worth—it's just the model's honest read on the odds. The good news? You've got time to shift this narrative: retake the LSAT for a boost, deepen your study game to climb those deciles next time, or seek scholarships to ease financial pressures. Many have turned similar profiles around and passed with flying colors. Keep pushing—you've got the fire; now channel it into what the model responds to most. We're rooting for your comeback story.
